mapping.a_element: distribution of party vote fractions across parties
mapping.b_element: spatial distribution of store locations/survival across Tokyo

brief_justification: Paper A's machinery is equilibrium statistical mechanics: coupled nonlinear oscillators reaching Rayleigh-Jeans thermalization and constraint-driven condensation under conservation of energy and norm, yielding Lorenz/Pareto curves. Paper B's machinery is a Bayesian spatial hierarchical model coupling a log-Gaussian Cox point process with a probit survival regression through a shared Gaussian-process field, fit by NNGP and Metropolis-within-Gibbs. Beyond both describing a spatial/statistical distribution, there is no role-for-role correspondence — no shared governing equation, threshold, or estimation principle — so the underlying methods differ.
